headline: A single sentence from WD-40's CFO explains how companies are greasing the wheels for disinflation
summary: WD-40's CFO, Sara Hyzer, revealed that the company is no longer planning "tactical price increases" in its major markets, indicating a shift towards disinflation. This comes as inflation slows and companies face pressure to justify price hikes, with some, like PepsiCo, already locking horns with retailers over pricing. This statement suggests a broader trend among companies moving away from rapid price increases seen during the pandemic era.
label: neutral

headline: Annual Report 2024
summary: Diageo's 2024 Annual Report highlights its position as a global leader in Total Beverage Alcohol (TBA) and its ambition to be a top-performing consumer products company. The report details financial and non-financial performance, strategic priorities for growth, and sustainability efforts under its "Spirit of Progress" plan. Key executives discuss challenges from the past year and the attractive long-term growth prospects of the TBA industry, particularly in spirits and premium segments.
label: negative

headline: Anheuser-Busch InBev SA/NV (BUD) Stock Forecast and Price Target 2025
summary: This article provides a stock forecast and price target for Anheuser-Busch InBev SA/NV (BUD) for 2025. Based on analyst ratings, BUD has a consensus rating of "Moderate Buy" with an average price target of $71.00, suggesting a potential upside of 16.57%. The article also details pros and cons for investors, analyst rating history, and comparisons to competitors.

headline: Keurig Dr Pepper to Buy Energy-Drink Maker Ghost for Over $1 Billion
summary: Keurig Dr Pepper is set to acquire energy-drink maker Ghost for over $1 billion. This marks Keurig Dr Pepper's largest deal since its 2018 acquisition of Dr Pepper Snapple Group. The deal was confirmed on Thursday morning following an earlier report by The Wall Street Journal.
label: positive

headline: PepsiCo releases 2023 ESG Summary highlighting PepsiCo Positive (pep+) results
summary: PepsiCo has released its 2023 ESG Summary, showcasing the company's progress and commitments under its PepsiCo Positive (pep+) strategic transformation. The report highlights results across various environmental, social, and governance initiatives, demonstrating PepsiCo's dedication to sustainability and positive impact. The summary provides insights into their efforts toward creating a more sustainable food system.
label: negative

headline: New Gatorade Hydration Booster electrolyte powder puts your water to work
summary: This press release from PepsiCo announces a new product: Gatorade Hydration Booster electrolyte powder. This product is designed to enhance water with electrolytes.
label: positive

headline: Top 100 local nonprofits across North America named Community Impact Award Winners by The PepsiCo Foundation
summary: The PepsiCo Foundation has recognized 100 local nonprofits across North America with Community Impact Awards. This initiative highlights PepsiCo's commitment to supporting impactful organizations within various communities. The awards celebrate the efforts of these nonprofits in creating positive change.
label: negative

headline: PepsiCo to replace Coca-Cola at Subway’s US locations
summary: PepsiCo Inc. has secured a 10-year agreement to become the exclusive beverage provider for all Subway sandwich shops in the US, replacing Coca-Cola Co. Coca-Cola brands will start to be phased out in January 2025. This deal also extends PepsiCo's Frito-Lay agreement to supply snacks to Subway through 2030, reinforcing PepsiCo's presence in one of the nation's largest restaurant chains.
label: negative

headline: Think you can do better? Doritos challenges fans to make a stronger Super Bowl Ad for a chance to win $1 Million
summary: Doritos is challenging fans to create a stronger Super Bowl ad for a chance to win $1 Million. This initiative invites consumers to participate in the brand's iconic advertising during one of the most-watched television events of the year. The contest aims to engage the public creatively and generate buzz for the brand.
label: positive

headline: Anaergia to Supply Organic Waste to Energy Solution to PepsiCo in Colombia
summary: Anaergia's Canadian subsidiary has secured a contract with PepsiCo Alimentos Z.F. LTDA to install high-efficiency digestion technology at PepsiCo's food production facility in Funza, Colombia. This system will convert approximately 50,000 tons of organic residues annually into renewable natural gas, significantly reducing the facility's carbon footprint by up to 3,700 tons of CO2 per year. This project marks Anaergia's third engagement with PepsiCo facilities across three continents, underscoring both companies' commitment to sustainability.
label: negative

headline: Flamin' Hot® teams up with Gabby Windey to help fans "Cheat on Flamin' Hot® Cheetos®"
summary: Flamin' Hot® has partnered with Gabby Windey to launch a campaign encouraging fans to try other Flamin' Hot® snacks beyond their usual Flamin' Hot® Cheetos®. This initiative aims to highlight the diverse range of Flamin' Hot® products available.
label: negative

headline: Coke, Pepsi, and Campbell Soup Yank Funding for Deadly Animal Experiments
summary: Following PETA's intervention, five major food and beverage companies—PepsiCo. Inc., The Coca-Cola Company, Campbell Soup Company, Fresh Innovations, and Sensient Technologies—have ceased funding for animal experimentation. The companies were informed that their contributions were supporting cruel experiments at the University of Wisconsin–Madison’s Food Research Institute. The Coca-Cola Company has completely withdrawn sponsorship, while the others have imposed restrictions to prevent their money from funding animal tests.
label: neutral

headline: PepsiCo: Q1 Earnings Snapshot
summary: PepsiCo reported strong first-quarter earnings of $2.04 billion, with adjusted earnings per share of $1.61, surpassing Wall Street's expectations. The food and beverage company's revenue also exceeded forecasts, reaching $18.25 billion for the period. PepsiCo anticipates full-year earnings to be $8.15 per share.
label: negative

headline: Why PepsiCo, Invitation Homes And S&T Bancorp Are Winners For Passive Income
summary: This article highlights PepsiCo, Invitation Homes, and S&T Bancorp as strong choices for passive income due to their consistent dividend increases and current yields over 3%. Each company has a history of rewarding shareholders with growing dividends, making them attractive to income-focused investors. The piece details their recent dividend announcements, financial performance, and market positions.
